features.label: Built-in AI Assistant (Ollama)
features.desc: A fully local, private AI assistant powered by Ollama. Click Assistant in the toolbar or press Ctrl+Shift+A to open the side panel. No cloud, no data leaving your machine.
features.howto: Click 'Assistant' in toolbar or press Ctrl+Shift+A. Requires Ollama running locally.

features.label: 34 MCP Browser Control Tools
features.desc: AI can control your browser — create/close/switch tabs, navigate, go back/forward, reload, read page content, scroll, zoom, find in page, manage bookmarks, history, downloads, take screenshots, toggle PiP, and search the web.
features.howto: Type 'Open YouTube' or 'What tabs are open?' — AI will automatically call the right tool.

features.label: Thinking Model Support
features.desc: Real-time <think> tag detection for models like DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3, Phi4. Spinner while thinking, then 'Thought for Xs' collapsible accordion when done. Click to expand/collapse reasoning.
features.howto: Select a thinking model (e.g. deepseek-r1) — reasoning will be shown separately.

features.label: Current Page Reading
features.desc: AI can read the current page content. Click the 📎 button to manually attach, or type 'summarize this page' — auto-detected. 8,000 char limit with clean text extraction.
features.howto: Click 📎 in the assistant input bar, or type 'summarize this page'.

features.label: Streaming Chat with Tool Cards
features.desc: Responses stream token-by-token with a stop button. Tool calls are shown as collapsible cards — auto-collapse on success, stay open on error. Follow-up response generated after tool execution.
features.howto: Send a message — response streams in real-time, tool calls appear as cards.

features.label: Model Selector with Live Status
features.desc: Available Ollama models are auto-fetched on panel open. Live status dot — green (online), red (offline), yellow (connecting). Refresh button to reload models. Default model saved across sessions.
features.howto: Open assistant panel — model dropdown at the top with live Ollama status.

features.label: Full Codebase Restructure
features.desc: The entire codebase reorganized into 130+ modular files. Settings panel: 4,289 lines → 65 modular files. Main process IPC: 1,164 lines → 13 handler files. Navigation, webview, tabs, features all modular.
features.howto: No user-facing changes — code is internally organized for better maintainability.

features.label: DevHub Lazy Loading
features.desc: 42 DevHub tool scripts no longer load at startup — only when DevHub button is clicked. App startup is ~35% faster (76 scripts instead of 118).
features.howto: App starts faster automatically. DevHub tools load on first open.

features.label: ⚠️ YouTube Ad Blocker Temporarily Removed
features.desc: The YouTube ad blocker has been temporarily removed. YouTube's advanced detection system detects DOM manipulation, playbackRate changes, and executeJavaScript injection — all triggered the 'Ad blockers violate YouTube's Terms of Service' popup. A better, undetectable solution is coming in v4.6.0.
features.howto: Improved YouTube ad blocking is expected in v4.6.0.

features.label: Storage path fix — passwords, permissions, addresses
features.desc: passwordHandlers, permissionHandlers, and addressHandlers were using the wrong storage path (vortex/storage/ instead of storage/). Fixed to use STORAGE_DIR from storage.js.

features.label: WhatsApp Panel crash on toolbar click
features.desc: The WhatsAppPanel module was missing after restructure, causing the toolbar button to crash on click. Module created and loaded correctly.

features.label: Storage:read duplicate handler crash on startup
features.desc: Both main.js and ipcHandler.js were registering storage IPC handlers, causing Electron to crash on startup with 'Attempted to register a second handler'. Fixed — only ipcHandler.js registers handlers.

features.label: Find in page not working after restructure
features.desc: A DOMContentLoaded wrapper prevented event listeners from binding after the restructure. Wrapper removed, listeners now bind directly on module load.
